preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiced palato-alveolar affricate is a process of assibilation and fortition, where a rhotic sound strengthens into an obstruent with an initial stop component.

examples.note: In many highland dialects (e.g. Ecuador, Bolivia), the rhotic is realized as a voiced fricative or affricate.
The historical palatalized rhotic (rz) assibilated into a voiced retroflex/palatal sound, which in some clusters is realized as an affricate.

sources: Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
Swan, O. E. (2002). A Grammar of Contemporary Polish.
